chunks.text: Kurt Keutzer is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 8020 Berkeley Way West and email keutzer@berkeley.edu, with office hours by appointment only. He is affiliated with the Berkeley AI Research (BAIR) lab and focuses on making Machine Learning and AI algorithms computationally efficient, particularly for Large Language Models and agentic systems. He invites postdoctoral fellows, PhD students, and outstanding undergraduates to apply via his personal webpage.
chunks.question: What is Kurt Keutzer's position and research focus?
chunks.answer: Professor Emeritus focusing on efficient AI algorithms.

chunks.text: Kurt Keutzer began his research career in industry, developing efficient algorithms for logic synthesis and winning awards like the 'Most Influential Paper of the 1980's' from the Design Automation Conference. He became the first CTO at Synopsys. His research is motivated by practical problems and aims for real-world impact, with his group entirely funded by industrial sponsors. He is actively engaged in entrepreneurship, co-founding Narada.ai and SigIQ.ai, and previously co-founded Deepscale (acquired by Tesla) and Nexusflow.ai (acquired by NVIDIA). He also serves as an investor or advisor to companies like Perplexity.ai and Sima.ai.
chunks.question: What are Kurt Keutzer's industry achievements and entrepreneurial activities?
chunks.answer: Award-winning logic synthesis research and multiple AI startup co-foundings.

chunks.text: Kurt Keutzer earned a PhD in Computer Science from Indiana University in 1984. His research areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), and Scientific Computing (SCI). He is associated with research centers such as the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) and Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD). He teaches CS 294-194: Research to Startup on Thursdays at Soda 310.
chunks.question: What are Kurt Keutzer's education and research areas?
chunks.answer: PhD in Computer Science, focusing on AI and architecture.

chunks.text: Kurt Keutzer's selected publications from 2016-2020 include works on efficient neural network training, quantization, and domain adaptation. Notable papers are 'Powernorm: Rethinking batch normalization in transformers' (ICML 2020), 'Zeroq: A novel zero shot quantization framework' (CVPR 2020), and 'Large batch optimization for deep learning: Training bert in 76 minutes' (ICLR 2020). Other publications cover topics like tensor rematerialization, adversarial attacks, and hardware-aware neural network design, often in collaboration with researchers like A. Gholami and M. W. Mahoney.
chunks.question: What topics do Kurt Keutzer's selected publications cover?
chunks.answer: Efficient neural network training and quantization techniques.

chunks.text: Kurt Keutzer has received several honors and awards, including the DAC's Most Influential Paper Award in 2023 and being named an Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Fellow in 1996. These recognitions highlight his contributions to design automation and electrical engineering fields over his career.
chunks.question: What awards has Kurt Keutzer received?
chunks.answer: DAC Most Influential Paper Award and IEEE Fellow.
